description: The Abe administration introduced controversial security legislation in 2015 to allow Japan's Self-Defense Forces to engage in collective self-defense. Despite mass public protests, the bills were passed by the Diet, fundamentally shifting Japan's post-war pacifist defense policy.

key_events.description: LDP submits security bills to the Diet for formal deliberation.
SEALDs (Students Emergency Action for Liberal Democracy) begin massive protests at the Diet.
Lower House passes the bills despite opposition walkouts.
Upper House officially enacts the legislation into law.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling public sentiment during executive-driven policy shifts that bypass widespread public consensus.
